Update from task 532054de-7976-408e-96db-bb1fa3e83201 (#2)

* Title: Complete BitTorrent client with download-only mode and piece management

Key features implemented:
- Added .gitignore file to exclude compiled Python files, logs, environment files, and editor directories
- Implemented PieceManager class in piece_manager.py to handle piece download progress, block assembly, and file writing with SHA1 verification
- Enhanced peer.py with full BitTorrent message protocol implementation including choke/unchoke/interested/have/bitfield/request/piece handling
- Modified peer.py to enforce download-only mode by ignoring incoming REQUEST messages and never sending PIECE responses
- Updated main.py to integrate piece management, add download monitoring with progress tracking, and implement concurrent peer connections
- Updated README.MD to reflect completion status with download-only functionality and seeding disabled

The application now provides a complete BitTorrent client that downloads files without uploading, implementing proper security measures to prevent seeding while maintaining full protocol compliance for downloading.

* update branch

* Update hive_core/peer.py

fix: equity updates immediately after trade close; fix collapse buttons for HA CSP

Issue 1 – Equity not updating on win/loss:
- bot.py: extract _refresh_equity_after_close() helper that refreshes
  equity in bot_status immediately when a trade closes, for BOTH paper
  and real trading modes. Previously equity only updated at the start of
  the next tick (paper: recalculates from DB; real: re-fetches exchange
  balance best-effort with graceful error handling).
- app.js: refreshBotStatus() now shows equity of the active symbol first,
  falling back to any non-null symbol if the active one has none yet.

Issue 2 – Collapse button not working in Home Assistant:
- index.html: remove inline onclick attributes from all three collapse
  buttons (CSP 'script-src: self' blocks inline event handlers in HA).
- app.js: wire btn-collapse-symbols, btn-collapse-params and
  btn-collapse-chart via addEventListener in DOMContentLoaded.

Sync trading-bot addon with main app (app.py, bot.py, database.py,
frontend/app.js, frontend/index.html, frontend/style.css in both
backend/ and trading-bot/backend/).

Tests: 233 passed (added 17 new tests covering paper/real equity update,
collapse button HTML/JS wiring, and equity API endpoint correctness).

fix(mcp): replace boolean flag with promise to prevent TOCTOU race in… (#390)

* fix(mcp): replace boolean flag with promise to prevent TOCTOU race in schema discovery

The _schemasDiscovering boolean allowed concurrent callers to both pass
the guard before either set it to true, causing duplicate mcpList() calls
and interleaved config mutations. Replace with a cached promise so
concurrent callers await the same in-flight discovery.

commit.message:
feat: fail-closed Prometheus role coverage, runtime hook enforcement, boundary checkpoints (#280)

Task 1 - Prometheus fail-closed role coverage gate:
- Replace fail-open skeleton injection in retry-failed and retry-no-output branches
  with per-missing-role telemetry + RolePlanCompletenessError throw
- Add ROLE_PLAN_COMPLETENESS_ERROR_CODE and RolePlanCompletenessError class
- Each missing role emits [PROMETHEUS][ROLE_PLAN_COVERAGE][FAIL_CLOSED] telemetry line
- Blocks Athena submission instead of silently injecting skeleton plans

Task 2 - Runtime hook decision enforcement:
- Add generateRuntimeHookDecisions() to policy_engine.ts — authoritative batch evaluator
- Add auditRuntimeHookEnforcement() to verification_gate.ts — consistency auditor
- Worker runtime now evaluates TOOL_INTENT envelopes against policy (not worker self-report)
- Remove [HOOK_DECISION] self-reporting obligation from worker prompt
- Import and wire both functions in worker_runner.ts

Task 3 - Checkpoint logical identifiers and boundary snapshots:
- Add CHECKPOINT_NS constants (planner/reviewer/dispatch/verification)

perf(licm): allow guard checks to be hoisted out of loops

Remove CheckMaps, CheckSmi, CheckNumber, CheckHeapObject, and all other
guard/checked-arithmetic nodes from the LICM is_pure exclusion list.

Guard checks only deoptimize on failure — they have no state-mutating
side effects. Hoisting them to the loop preheader is safe because:
- If the guard passes at loop entry, it passes for all iterations
  (shapes/types don't change without stores, which are already blocked)
- If the guard fails, we deopt immediately (cheaper than looping first)

This is critical for property_access benchmarks: CheckMaps was blocking
LoadNamedGeneric from being hoisted, keeping 5 property loads inside the
loop body instead of the preheader.

fix(attributeset): fix use-after-free in batch attribute removal (#1829)

AttributeSet_RemoveIdx processed removal indices from lowest to highest
(ascending). The swap-with-last pattern copies the value at position l-1
into the slot being removed. When l-1 itself is a removal target, the
copied value (including any heap pointer) becomes dangling when that
higher position is freed in a later iteration.

Reverse the loop direction to process highest indices first. This ensures
the swap source (l-1) is always a position that has already been processed
or is not scheduled for removal, preventing use-after-free and double-free
of heap-owning attribute values (strings, arrays, maps).

Bug was introduced in commit 0ddddf039 ("Reduce attribute-set memory
consumption") which added batch removal support.

Add regression test that batch-removes two and three heap-owning string
attributes including the last slot, verifying surviving values remain
valid and no crash occurs during cleanup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

r600: add Lima PP (Mali-400) reference corpus and analysis

14 source files from Mesa src/gallium/drivers/lima/ir/pp/ covering the
full Mali-400 pixel pipeline compiler: scheduler, packer, register
allocator, codegen, liveness, and compaction passes.

Includes 1176-line LIMA_PP_SCHEDULER_ANALYSIS.md mapping all 10 Lima
patterns to TeraScale: Sarkar reg_pressure formula, 11-constraint
cascade, source reordering for bypass eligibility, constant dedup.

Critical finding: Lima MUL→ADD bypass is intra-instruction (same VLIW
word) while TeraScale PV/PS is inter-bundle (group N→N+1), proving
C4/C5 must be interleaved, not sequential passes.
